questions.question: She ___ a student.
questions.explanation_pt: Com she/he/it usa-se 'is'. I am, You are, She is.

questions.question: What is 'dog' in Portuguese?
questions.explanation_pt: 'Dog' = cachorro. 'Cat' = gato, 'Bird' = pássaro, 'Fish' = peixe.

questions.question: I ___ from Brazil.
questions.explanation_pt: Com 'I' sempre usa-se 'am'. I am = Eu sou/estou.

questions.question: The opposite of 'hot' is:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Hot' (quente) × 'Cold' (frio). São antônimos.

questions.question: ___ you like coffee?
questions.explanation_pt: Perguntas com 'you' no presente: Do you...? Does é para he/she/it.

questions.question: Which word means 'casa'?
questions.explanation_pt: 'House' = casa. 'Car' = carro, 'Tree' = árvore, 'Book' = livro.

questions.question: They ___ happy today.
questions.explanation_pt: Com 'they' usa-se 'are'. They are = Eles são/estão.

questions.question: He ___ to school every day.
questions.explanation_pt: Presente simples com he/she/it: o verbo recebe -s ou -es. He goes.

questions.question: 'Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday' are:
questions.explanation_pt: São dias da semana (days of the week). Months = meses, Colors = cores.

questions.question: I ___ not like spiders.
questions.explanation_pt: Negativa com 'I': I do not (don't) like. Com he/she: does not (doesn't).

questions.question: She ___ to the cinema last night.
questions.explanation_pt: 'Last night' = passado. Past simple de 'go' = 'went' (irregular).

questions.question: 'I'm looking for a job' means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Look for' = procurar. 'I'm looking for a job' = Estou procurando um emprego.

questions.question: This book is ___ than that one.
questions.explanation_pt: Comparativo de adjetivos longos: more + adjetivo. More interesting = mais interessante.

questions.question: I ___ never been to Japan.
questions.explanation_pt: Present perfect com 'I': I have + particípio. I have never been = Eu nunca fui/estive.

questions.question: 'Expensive' means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Expensive' = caro. 'Cheap' = barato. Cuidado: NÃO é falso cognato.

questions.question: There ___ many people at the party.
questions.explanation_pt: 'People' é plural, então usa 'were' (passado de 'are'). There were = Havia/Tinha.

questions.question: 'I usually wake up at 7, have breakfast, and take the bus to work.' What does this person do in the morning?
options: A) Drives to work after lunch
B) Wakes up, eats, and takes the bus to work
C) Walks to school every morning
D) Sleeps until noon on weekdays
questions.explanation_pt: O texto descreve: acordar às 7, tomar café, pegar ônibus para o trabalho.

questions.question: She ___ English since 2020.
questions.explanation_pt: 'Since 2020' indica ação que começou no passado e continua: Present Perfect. She has studied.

questions.question: A person who teaches at a university is a:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Professor' = professor universitário. Student = aluno, Doctor = médico, Lawyer = advogado.

questions.question: If it rains, I ___ at home.
questions.explanation_pt: First conditional: If + present, will + infinitive. If it rains, I will stay.

questions.question: If I ___ more money, I would travel the world.
questions.explanation_pt: Second conditional: If + past simple, would + infinitive. Situação hipotética no presente.

questions.question: 'Despite the rain, the match continued.' 'Despite' means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Despite' = apesar de (in spite of). Indica contraste. Diferente de 'because of' (por causa de).

questions.question: The letter ___ yesterday.
questions.explanation_pt: Passive voice no passado: was/were + particípio. The letter was sent = A carta foi enviada.

questions.question: 'The government announced a new policy to reduce carbon emissions by 40% by 2030. Environmental groups welcomed the decision but said it did not go far enough.' What is the environmental groups' position?
options: A) They completely oppose the new policy and want it reversed
B) They support it but think it should be more ambitious
C) They believe 40% reduction is too aggressive a target
D) They have not commented on the government's announcement
questions.explanation_pt: 'Welcomed but said it did not go far enough' = apoiam mas acham que deveria ser mais ambicioso.

questions.question: She asked me ___ I had been to London.
questions.explanation_pt: Reported questions (yes/no): She asked me if/whether... Quando a pergunta original é sim/não, usa-se 'if'.

questions.question: 'The company launched a new product.' 'Launched' is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Launch' = lançar/introduzir (um produto, projeto, campanha). Diferente de 'cancel' (cancelar).

questions.question: I wish I ___ speak French fluently.
questions.explanation_pt: Wish + past simple/could: expressa desejo sobre algo irreal no presente. I wish I could = Quem dera eu pudesse.

questions.question: The book ___ she recommended was excellent.
questions.explanation_pt: Relative pronoun para coisas: which/that. 'Who' é para pessoas. The book which she recommended...

questions.question: 'Reliable' means someone or something that:
options: B) Can be trusted and depended on consistently
C) Is very expensive and luxurious
D) Is old and no longer functional
questions.explanation_pt: 'Reliable' = confiável, de confiança. Uma pessoa reliable é alguém em quem você pode confiar.

questions.question: By the time I arrived, the meeting ___.
questions.explanation_pt: Past perfect: had + particípio. Ação anterior a outra no passado. A reunião já tinha começado quando cheguei.

questions.question: Not only ___ the exam, but she also got the highest score.
questions.explanation_pt: Inversão após 'Not only': Not only did she pass... Estrutura enfática que inverte auxiliar e sujeito.

questions.question: 'The findings of the study corroborate previous research.' 'Corroborate' means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Corroborate' = confirmar, corroborar, dar suporte. Vocabulário acadêmico frequente no TOEFL.

questions.question: 'While correlation between social media use and anxiety has been documented, establishing causation remains problematic. Longitudinal studies suggest the relationship may be bidirectional.' The author's main point is:
options: A) Social media definitely causes anxiety in all users
B) There is no relationship between social media and anxiety
C) The causal direction of the relationship is not yet clear
D) Longitudinal studies have proven social media is safe
questions.explanation_pt: 'Establishing causation remains problematic' + 'bidirectional' = a direção causal não está clara.

questions.question: Had I known about the deadline, I ___ my application earlier.
questions.explanation_pt: Third conditional invertido: Had I known = If I had known. Would have + particípio = resultado hipotético no passado.

questions.question: 'The policy was implemented despite considerable opposition.' 'Considerable' is closest to:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Considerable' = significativo, considerável. 'Considerable opposition' = oposição significativa.

questions.question: It's high time we ___ a decision about the project.
questions.explanation_pt: 'It's high time' + past simple: expressa que algo deveria ter sido feito já. It's high time we made...

questions.question: 'The proliferation of misinformation online has prompted calls for greater digital literacy education. Proponents argue that teaching critical evaluation of sources should be integrated into school curricula from an early age.' The word 'proliferation' most likely means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Proliferation' = proliferação, disseminação rápida. Contexto: 'misinformation online has prompted calls' indica aumento.

questions.question: The report, ___ was published last week, has generated significant debate.
questions.explanation_pt: Non-defining relative clause (com vírgulas): usa 'which', nunca 'that'. Adiciona informação extra.

questions.question: 'The government allocated substantial funds to infrastructure.' 'Allocated' means:
options: B) Distributed for a specific purpose
questions.explanation_pt: 'Allocate' = alocar, destinar, distribuir para um fim específico. Vocabulário acadêmico/político.

questions.question: Rarely ___ such a comprehensive analysis of the issue.
questions.explanation_pt: Inversão após advérbios negativos: Rarely/Seldom/Never + auxiliar + sujeito. Rarely have we seen...

questions.question: Scarcely ___ the building when the fire alarm went off.
questions.explanation_pt: Inversão com 'Scarcely...when': Scarcely had he left when... = Mal ele tinha saído quando...

questions.question: 'The researcher's methodology was deemed fundamentally flawed, thereby undermining the validity of the entire study.' 'Undermining' means:
options: B) Weakening or subverting from the foundation
questions.explanation_pt: 'Undermine' = minar, enfraquecer pela base. Vocabulário acadêmico avançado essencial no TOEFL.

questions.question: 'The ostensible purpose of the legislation was to protect consumer privacy; however, critics contend that its vague provisions may paradoxically facilitate greater governmental surveillance.' The author suggests that:
options: A) The legislation will definitely protect consumer privacy as intended
B) Critics are wrong to question the legislation's effectiveness
C) The law's stated aim may differ from its actual consequences
D) Governmental surveillance is beneficial for consumer protection
questions.explanation_pt: 'Ostensible' (aparente) + 'paradoxically facilitate' = o objetivo declarado pode diferir das consequências reais.

questions.question: ___ it not been for her intervention, the negotiations would have collapsed.
questions.explanation_pt: Third conditional invertido formal: Had it not been for = If it had not been for. Estilo acadêmico/formal.

questions.question: 'The findings are commensurate with those of previous studies.' 'Commensurate' means:
options: C) Proportionate to and consistent with
questions.explanation_pt: 'Commensurate' = proporcional a, compatível com. Vocabulário acadêmico de nível C1.

questions.question: 'Notwithstanding the methodological limitations acknowledged by the authors, the study's contribution to the field of epigenetics cannot be overstated. The longitudinal design, encompassing three generations of participants, provides unprecedented insight into transgenerational inheritance patterns.' The phrase 'cannot be overstated' suggests the contribution is:
options: A) Frequently exaggerated by the media
questions.explanation_pt: 'Cannot be overstated' = não pode ser exagerado = é extremamente importante. Expressão acadêmica formal.

questions.question: It is imperative that the committee ___ its decision by Friday.
questions.explanation_pt: Subjunctive: It is imperative/essential/vital that + sujeito + base form (sem -s). Formal/acadêmico.

questions.question: 'The government's austerity measures have exacerbated economic inequality.' 'Exacerbated' means:
questions.explanation_pt: 'Exacerbate' = exacerbar, piorar significativamente. Frequente em textos acadêmicos e TOEFL.

questions.question: So complex ___ the problem that even experts struggled to find a solution.
questions.explanation_pt: Inversão enfática: So + adjetivo + was/is + sujeito + that... Estilo formal/acadêmico.

questions.question: 'The two theories, once thought to be mutually exclusive, have been shown to be complementary.' 'Mutually exclusive' means:
options: B) Impossible to be true at the same time
C) Based on the same fundamental principles
D) Developed by the same research team
questions.explanation_pt: 'Mutually exclusive' = mutuamente exclusivos, não podem coexistir. Se um é verdade, o outro não pode ser.
